profile.description: A cross-platform PowerShell-based agent tool — a drop-in replacement for agentsh (bash) with identical sandboxing, permissions, and security features, targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS.

goals.description: Build a drop-in PowerShell replacement for agentsh with identical capabilities and security model
Cross-platform support: Windows, Linux, and macOS
Implement sandboxing and permissions system equivalent to agentsh
Secure command execution with configurable permission boundaries
Run PowerShell scripts and commands in the same workflow as agentsh runs bash

architecture.dataFlow: Not yet established - project is in initial planning phase

workflows.name: release
triggers: manual or automated after CI pass

naming.powershell: PascalCase for cmdlets and functions, Approved Verbs (Get-, Set-, New-, etc.)
naming.typescript: camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for types/classes

conventions.testingConventions: Pester tests required for all PowerShell modules and functions
additionalRules: Cross-platform CI must run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
PowerShell best practices per community style guide
Strict TypeScript with no-any for all TS code

changesSummary: Initial profile creation from scratch. Merged repo analysis (new project scaffold), tools analysis (no existing tools), process mining (2 commits, greenfield), and user interview (drop-in agentsh replacement, trunk-based workflow, autonomous babysitter, PowerShell best practices + strict TS + Pester tests + cross-platform CI).
